titleStmt.title: SII 18.20: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 20. (B.K. No. 22 of 1934-35.) HIRĒMĀGANŪR, RANEBENNUR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Pillar set up in front of the temple of Kallēśvara.

summary.p: This inscription refers itself to the reign of Indara Vallabha (Indra III) and is dated in Śaka 837, Īśvara, Kārtika, śu. Pūrṇimā, Lunar eclipse. Taking the cyclic year to be of the northern cycle, the date corresponds to A.D. 916, October 13, Sunday. On this day there was a lunar eclipse. It records the gift of a chitra-gōsāsa made by a certain gauṇḍa (name lost) with the blessings of the Mahājanas of Keṛevūr and Tāṇagundūr when Baṁkeya was governing Banavāsi-nāḍu and Āychaṇṇa was the nāḻgāmuṇḍa of Kuḍuvaṇṇegaṇḍa Seventy and Iṭṭige Thirty. It also refers to the construction of tanks and a temple and grant of money. It was written by Dēvaṇṇa. Mentions Māgundura (present Hirēmāganūr ?).

In another inscription belonging to the reign of Indra III (No. 23 below) a Baṅkeya belonging to the Chellakētana family is mentioned as governing Banavāsināḍu. It is quite likely that Baṅkeya of the present record is the same as Baṅkeya of the Chellakētana family.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī sakanṛpakāḷākrāntasaṁbatsaraṁgaḷeṇṭanūṟamūva
ttēḻaneya Īśvaravarṣaṁ pravartise Indharavallabha pṛtuvīrā
jyaṁgeyye baṁkeyaṁ banavāsināḍanāḷe kuḍuvaṇnagaṇḍame
ḻpattu Iṭṭigemūvattarkke Āycaṇna nāḻgāmuṇḍugeyyuttire
kārttika māsa suddhapakṣa paurṇnamāse śōmagrahaṇadoḷmāgunduraThe letter ka is written above the line in smaller characters.
koṇnanda gavuṇḍa keṟevūra tāṇagundūra mahājana
da parakeyoḷ citragōsaśamiḻderaḍu keṟeya kaṭṭi bhīma
rgge dakṣiṇe saṁpūrṇnamāge
ḷnūṟu paṇaṁ Uttarāyaṇa viṣu
ḷondu gadyāṇaṁ sūryasōmagrahaṇadoḷdānaṁ geydinitu dharma
ḷasama goṭṭu petta basiḻoḷpaṭṭamaṁ kaṭṭidōṁ A
lliṁ baḻike liṁgapratiṣThe geydu dēgulava māḍisi kaḷami
rmma Abhivṛddhi sandudu dēvayya baredōṁ maṁgaḷa ©
Digital edition of SII 18.20 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
